# RedwoodSDK: React 19 Metadata Tags ## Background A RedwoodSDK project is pre-installed at `/home/user/myapp`. React 19 hoists `` and `<meta>` components into the document head automatically. Use that mechanism so each page renders its own page-specific metadata, while the `Document` shell stays generic. ## Requirements Implement page server components that render `<title>` and `<meta>` tags inline. Do **not** hard-code page-specific titles in the `Document` component. ## Acceptance Criteria * Project path: /home/user/myapp * Start command: npm run dev -- --host 0.0.0.0 --port 5173 * Port: 5173 * Routes (all under the existing `render(Document, ...)` block): * `GET /` → final HTML `<head>` must contain `<title>Home — rwsdk` and `Home`. * `GET /about` → `` must contain `About — rwsdk` and `About`. * `GET /docs` → `` contains `Docs — rwsdk`, `Docs`.

**Exploring Project Structure**

I'm beginning by investigating the project's file structure at `/home/user/myapp` to understand where to integrate the React 19 metadata tags. My initial approach is to list or search for relevant files within this directory.

**Listing Files Now**

I'm proceeding with listing the files within the `/home/user/myapp` directory. Before diving deeper, I'm also considering whether creating a preliminary todo list might streamline the subsequent steps of exploring the codebase and identifying routing or document files.

todos.content: Explore the codebase at /home/user/myapp to locate the router, Document, and page components
Modify page components to render page-specific metadata inline (<title> and <meta> tags)
Verify Document component doesn't hard-code page-specific titles
Start the dev server and verify the routes and their rendered metadata

part.text:
**Exploring File Structure**

I'm currently examining the files within `/home/user/myapp/src/app` and `/home/user/myapp/src/app/pages`. My focus is on understanding how routing and document rendering are implemented, particularly in conjunction with `worker.tsx` and `client.tsx`.

**Analyzing Component Rendering**

I'm currently examining how `worker.tsx` instantiates the `Document` component and how routes are managed within that block. My focus is on verifying that the `<head>` section of the generated HTML correctly reflects the title "Home — rwsdk" and includes the specified meta description for the root route.

output.content:
export const Document: React.FC<{ children: React.ReactNode }> = ({
  children,
}) => (
  <html lang="en">
    <head>
      <meta charSet="utf-8" />
      <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1" />
      <title>@redwoodjs/starter-minimal</title>
      <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com" />
      <link
        rel="preconnect"
        href="https://fonts.gstatic.com"
        crossOrigin=""
      />
      <link
        rel="stylesheet"
        href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Noto+Sans:ital,wght@0,100..900;1,100..900&family=Playfair+Display:ital,wght@0,400..900;1,400..900&display=optional"
        precedence="first"
      />
      <link rel="modulepreload" href="/src/client.tsx" />
    </head>
    <body>
      {children}
      <script>import("/src/client.tsx")</script>
    </body>
  </html>
);

part.text:
**Updating Document Title**

I'm currently reviewing the `Document` component. My focus is on eliminating any hardcoded page titles, like the `@redwoodjs/starter-minimal` example I found, to ensure greater flexibility.

**Refining Home Page** My current focus is on enriching the `Home` component. Beyond just rendering ``, I'm integrating the specific meta tags and title required for the `/` endpoint's final HTML, ensuring the correct `` content is delivered as per the specifications. **Finalizing Meta Tags** I'm now focusing on the specific meta tags and title for the home page, ensuring `Home — rwsdk` and the correct meta description are in place. My current consideration is whether the body should *only* contain `

Home

` or if it simply needs to include it alongside other content. I'm leaning towards clean page components.

{
  "name": "@redwoodjs/starter",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "description": "A bare-bones RedwoodSDK starter",
  "main": "index.js",
  "type": "module",
  "keywords": [],
  "author": "",
  "license": "MIT",
  "private": true,
  "scripts": {
    "build": "vite build",
    "dev": "vite dev",
    "dev:init": "rw-scripts dev-init",
    "preview": "vite preview",
    "worker:run": "rw-scripts worker-run",
    "clean": "npm run clean:vite",
    "clean:vite": "rm -rf ./node_modules/.vite",
    "release": "rw-scripts ensure-deploy-env && npm run clean && npm run build && wrangler deploy",
    "generate": "rw-scripts ensure-env && wrangler types --include-runtime false",
    "check": "npm run generate && npm run types",
    "types": "tsc"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "react": "19.2.6",
    "react-dom": "19.2.6",
    "react-server-dom-webpack": "19.2.6",

      "sharp",

  }
}

part.text:
**Analyzing Client Initialization**

I'm currently examining `client.tsx` to understand its role in client-side application hydration. My focus has shifted to a closer inspection of `worker.tsx` to clarify its integration within the routing and app definition processes, particularly with `rwsdk/router` and `rwsdk/worker`.

**Defining App Routes**

My thoughts are now focused on ensuring all defined routes, specifically `/`, `/about`, and `/docs`, are correctly nested within the `render(Document, ...)` block as per the acceptance criteria. I'm verifying the meta tag content for each, including titles, descriptions, and the Open Graph image for `/docs`.

**Refining Home Page Meta**

I'm clarifying the exact character for the title separator in the `<title>` tag, specifically for the home page. My current focus is on ensuring the correct em dash (`—`) is used as per the prompt's specifications for titles and meta descriptions, before proceeding to define the About and Docs components.

**Confirming Title Characters**

I've confirmed all page titles utilize the em dash (`—`) as specified. My attention is now on `src/app/document.tsx` to ensure page-specific titles aren't hardcoded there, aiming to remove the generic title element entirely to facilitate dynamic title rendering.

part.text:
**Rendering Home Page Content**

I've successfully updated `src/app/pages/home.tsx` to include rendering for the home page's metadata and body. I'm now reviewing the `Welcome` component's integration within the `Home` component.

**Generating Page Components**

I'm currently focusing on generating the necessary page components. I've decided to create an `About` component in `src/app/pages/about.tsx` and a `Docs` component in `src/app/pages/docs.tsx`. My next step is to use `writeToFile` to define the content for `src/app/pages/about.tsx` and then verify it against the `GET /about` requirements, specifically focusing on the `<head>` section.

**Generating Docs Page**

I am now focused on creating the `docs.tsx` file. My current task involves ensuring the `<head>` section accurately includes the specified title, description, and Open Graph image meta tags for the `/docs` route.

**Implementing Routing Logic**

I'm currently focused on integrating the new routes within `src/worker.tsx`. The plan is to import the necessary components and then leverage the `route` function from `rwsdk/router` to define their corresponding paths. This will enable seamless navigation within the application.

**Updating the Todo List**

I've just updated our internal todo list. The exploration of the codebase, modifications to page components, and verification that the Document component avoids hard-coded titles are now marked as complete. This reflects our recent progress.

Modify page components to render page-specific metadata inline (<title> and <meta> tags)
Verify Document component doesn't hard-code page-specific titles
Start the dev server and verify the routes and their rendered metadata

input.result:
I have successfully implemented page-specific React 19 metadata tags in the RedwoodSDK project at `/home/user/myapp`. Here is a summary of the changes made: 1. **Generic Document Shell**: Modified `src/app/document.tsx` to remove the hard-coded `` tag, allowing page components to hoist their own metadata dynamically via React 19's metadata hoisting mechanism. 2. **Page Components Metadata**: - **Home (`/`)**: Updated `src/app/pages/home.tsx` to render `<title>Home — rwsdk`, ``, and `

Home

` inline. - **About (`/about`)**: Created `src/app/pages/about.tsx` rendering `About — rwsdk`, ``, and `

About

` inline. - **Docs (`/docs`)**: Created `src/app/pages/docs.tsx` rendering `Docs — rwsdk`, ``, ``, and `

Docs

` inline. 3. **Router Integration**: Added the new routes under the existing `render(Document, ...)` block in `src/worker.tsx`. 4. **Verification**: Successfully ran the TypeScript type checker, built the production bundles, started the Vite dev server, and verified via `curl` that all three routes render their respective title and meta tags hoisted correctly inside the HTML ``, with the correct `

` tags in the body.
